description: CodeQuest Forge Ada curriculum: 1,200 repetition-driven quests across 120 senior-development concepts.

quests.focus: creating, building, running, cleaning, and inspecting an Alire crate
quests.title: Recall: Alire crate lifecycle
quests.summary: Recall practice for Alire crate lifecycle, emphasizing creating, building, running, cleaning, and inspecting an Alire crate.
quests.prompt: Define Alire crate lifecycle without looking it up. State the Ada rule, the problem it solves, and one misuse to avoid. Focus on creating, building, running, cleaning, and inspecting an Alire crate.
instructions: Work without copying a finished solution first.
Use Alire for every code-producing quest.
Record what the compiler, runtime, tests, or reviewer actually showed.
acceptance: Give an accurate definition.
State the important Ada-specific rule.
Name one realistic misuse or failure mode.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
rubric: Give an accurate definition.
State the important Ada-specific rule.
Name one realistic misuse or failure mode.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
quests.alire_commands:
mkdir -p ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery/ada-tooling-foundations
cd ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery/ada-tooling-foundations
alr init --bin 0001-alire-crate-lifecycle-recall
cd 0001-alire-crate-lifecycle-recall
alr build
alr run

quests.focus: creating, building, running, cleaning, and inspecting an Alire crate
quests.title: Explain: Alire crate lifecycle
quests.summary: Explain practice for Alire crate lifecycle, emphasizing creating, building, running, cleaning, and inspecting an Alire crate.
quests.prompt: Explain Alire crate lifecycle to a competent C++ developer who is new to Ada. Compare the closest C++ idea, then explain where the comparison breaks down. Focus on creating, building, running, cleaning, and inspecting an Alire crate.
instructions: Work without copying a finished solution first.
Use Alire for every code-producing quest.
Record what the compiler, runtime, tests, or reviewer actually showed.
acceptance: Use your own words.
Include a useful C++ comparison.
Identify at least one Ada behavior that differs from C++.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
rubric: Use your own words.
Include a useful C++ comparison.
Identify at least one Ada behavior that differs from C++.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.

quests.prompt: Create or locate a minimal compiling Ada example that demonstrates Identifiers and naming. Annotate every line related to the concept and explain how the compiler enforces Ada identifier rules, readability, casing conventions, and semantic names.
instructions: Work without copying a finished solution first.
Use Alire for every code-producing quest.
Record what the compiler, runtime, tests, or reviewer actually showed.
acceptance: The example builds with Alire.
Every relevant declaration and statement is explained.
The explanation identifies compile-time and runtime checks.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
rubric: The example builds with Alire.
Every relevant declaration and statement is explained.
The explanation identifies compile-time and runtime checks.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.

alr init --bin 0023-identifiers-and-naming-read
cd 0023-identifiers-and-naming-read
alr build
alr run

quests.prompt: Explain Derived types to a competent C++ developer who is new to Ada. Compare the closest C++ idea, then explain where the comparison breaks down. Focus on creating semantically distinct types and controlling implicit compatibility.
instructions: Work without copying a finished solution first.
Use Alire for every code-producing quest.
Record what the compiler, runtime, tests, or reviewer actually showed.
acceptance: Use your own words.
Include a useful C++ comparison.
Identify at least one Ada behavior that differs from C++.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
rubric: Use your own words.
Include a useful C++ comparison.
Identify at least one Ada behavior that differs from C++.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.

cd ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery/ada-scalar-types
alr init --bin 0182-derived-types-explain
cd 0182-derived-types-explain
alr build
alr run

quests.prompt: Create or locate a minimal compiling Ada example that demonstrates Unconstrained arrays. Annotate every line related to the concept and explain how the compiler enforces array type definitions, bounds carried by objects, and flexible parameters.
instructions: Work without copying a finished solution first.
Use Alire for every code-producing quest.
Record what the compiler, runtime, tests, or reviewer actually showed.
acceptance: The example builds with Alire.
Every relevant declaration and statement is explained.
The explanation identifies compile-time and runtime checks.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
rubric: The example builds with Alire.
Every relevant declaration and statement is explained.
The explanation identifies compile-time and runtime checks.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
quests.alire_commands:
mkdir -p ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery/ada-composite-access
cd ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery/ada-composite-access
alr init --bin 0413-unconstrained-arrays-read
cd 0413-unconstrained-arrays-read
alr build
alr run

quests.focus: array type definitions, bounds carried by objects, and flexible parameters
quests.title: Predict: Unconstrained arrays
quests.summary: Predict practice for Unconstrained arrays, emphasizing array type definitions, bounds carried by objects, and flexible parameters.
quests.prompt: Write a small Ada program involving Unconstrained arrays. Before building it, predict the compiler result and runtime behavior. Build and run it, then explain every difference between prediction and observation. Focus on array type definitions, bounds carried by objects, and flexible parameters.
instructions: Work without copying a finished solution first.
Use Alire for every code-producing quest.
Record what the compiler, runtime, tests, or reviewer actually showed.
acceptance: Record the prediction before compiling.
Capture the actual compiler or runtime result.
Explain any mismatch rather than merely correcting it.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
rubric: Record the prediction before compiling.
Capture the actual compiler or runtime result.
Explain any mismatch rather than merely correcting it.

quests.prompt: Explain Formal types to a competent C++ developer who is new to Ada. Compare the closest C++ idea, then explain where the comparison breaks down. Focus on discrete, private, tagged, access, array, and numeric generic formal categories.
instructions: Work without copying a finished solution first.
Use Alire for every code-producing quest.
Record what the compiler, runtime, tests, or reviewer actually showed.
acceptance: Use your own words.
Include a useful C++ comparison.
Identify at least one Ada behavior that differs from C++.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
rubric: Use your own words.
Include a useful C++ comparison.
Identify at least one Ada behavior that differs from C++.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.

alr build

quests.prompt: Explain Coverage and mutation thinking to a competent C++ developer who is new to Ada. Compare the closest C++ idea, then explain where the comparison breaks down. Focus on statement, decision, MC/DC goals, test gaps, and deliberate fault seeding.
instructions: Work without copying a finished solution first.
Use Alire for every code-producing quest.
Record what the compiler, runtime, tests, or reviewer actually showed.
acceptance: Use your own words.
Include a useful C++ comparison.
Identify at least one Ada behavior that differs from C++.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
rubric: Use your own words.
Include a useful C++ comparison.
Identify at least one Ada behavior that differs from C++.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.

alr run

quests.prompt: Design a production-quality package or subsystem that depends on Timers, background work, packaging, and accessibility. Specify the public API, hidden representation, contracts, error policy, tests, and dependency direction. Emphasize idle callbacks, task coordination, UI-thread safety, deployment, shortcuts, and accessible labels.
instructions: Work without copying a finished solution first.
Use Alire for every code-producing quest.
Record what the compiler, runtime, tests, or reviewer actually showed.
Keep domain logic outside GtkAda callback bodies so it can be tested independently.
acceptance: Public and private responsibilities are separated.
Contracts and error behavior are explicit.
The design is testable without its UI, network, or hardware adapter.
Save a short note describing what you would recognize faster next time.
rubric: Public and private responsibilities are separated.

quests.alire_commands:

cd 1199-timers-background-work-packaging-and-accessibility
alr build
alr run
